_setupSafe().details: Sets up a Safe with default parameters. The SafeInstance will have 3 owners and a threshold of 2.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256).details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256,uint256).details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.initialBalance: The initial balance of the Safe.
params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256,uint256,(bo.details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.advancedParams: The advanced parameters for the Safe initialization.
params.initialBalance: The initial balance of the Safe.
params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

getSafe(address).details: Returns the Safe instance with the given address.

params._safe: The address of the Safe instance to return.

returns._0: The Safe instance with the given address.

testDiff_getSignaturesVsCheckSignatures_.details: Test that for a given set of signatures:      1. safe.checkNSignatures() succeeds      2. the getSigners() method returns the expected signers      3. the expected signers are all owners of the safe.      Demonstrating these three properties is sufficient to prove that the getSigners() method      returns the same signatures as those recovered by safe.checkNSignatures().

_setupSafe().details: Sets up a Safe with default parameters. The SafeInstance will have 3 owners and a threshold of 2.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256).details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256,uint256).details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.initialBalance: The initial balance of the Safe.
params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256,uint256,(bo.details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.advancedParams: The advanced parameters for the Safe initialization.
params.initialBalance: The initial balance of the Safe.
params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

getSafe(address).details: Returns the Safe instance with the given address.

params._safe: The address of the Safe instance to return.

returns._0: The Safe instance with the given address.

testDiff_getSignaturesVsCheckSignatures_.details: Test that for a given set of signatures:      1. safe.checkNSignatures() succeeds      2. the getSigners() method returns the expected signers      3. the expected signers are all owners of the safe.      Demonstrating these three properties is sufficient to prove that the getSigners() method      returns the same signatures as those recovered by safe.checkNSignatures().

documentation.text:
@dev Test that for a given set of signatures:
      1. safe.checkNSignatures() succeeds
      2. the getSigners() method returns the expected signers
      3. the expected signers are all owners of the safe.
      Demonstrating these three properties is sufficient to prove that the getSigners() method
      returns the same signatures as those recovered by safe.checkNSignatures().
